---
title: "Forecasts"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from pathlib import Path
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

data_dir = Path("../data/processed")

try:
    forecasts = pd.read_parquet(data_dir / "forecasts.parquet").copy()
    forecasts["date"] = pd.to_datetime(forecasts["date"])
    forecasts = forecasts.sort_values("date")
except:
    forecasts = pd.DataFrame()

```

## All Upcoming Fixtures

```{python}
#| echo: false
if len(forecasts) > 0:
    display_df = forecasts[[
        "date", "home_team", "away_team", "matchday",
        "predicted_attendance", "lower_bound", "upper_bound"
    ]].copy()

    display_df["date"] = display_df["date"].dt.strftime("%b %d, %Y")
    display_df["predicted_attendance"] = display_df["predicted_attendance"].round(0).astype(int)
    display_df["lower_bound"] = display_df["lower_bound"].round(0).astype(int)
    display_df["upper_bound"] = display_df["upper_bound"].round(0).astype(int)
    display_df.columns = ["Date", "Home Team", "Away Team", "Matchday", "Predicted", "Lower Bound", "Upper Bound"]

    # Use itables for interactive table
    from itables import show
    show(display_df, maxRows=20)
else:
    print("No forecasts available")

```

## Attendance Forecast with Error Bars

```{python}
#| echo: false
if len(forecasts) > 0:
    plot_df = forecasts.copy()
    plot_df["fixture"] = plot_df["home_team"] + " vs " + plot_df["away_team"]
    plot_df = plot_df.sort_values("predicted_attendance", ascending=False).head(15)

    fig = px.scatter(
        plot_df,
        x="fixture",
        y="predicted_attendance",
        error_y="upper_bound",
        title="Attendance Forecasts with 80% Prediction Intervals (Top 15)",
        labels={"predicted_attendance": "Predicted Attendance"},
        hover_data={"date": True, "home_team": True}
    )
    fig.add_scatter(
        x=plot_df["fixture"],
        y=plot_df["lower_bound"],
        mode='markers',
        marker=dict(color='rgba(0,0,0,0)'),
        showlegend=False,
        name="Lower Bound"
    )
    fig.update_layout(height=500, xaxis_tickangle=-45)
    fig.show()
else:
    print("No forecast data available")

```
